notes.en: Become especially serious during dry periods and under protected cultivation. Overuse of broad-spectrum insecticides eliminates parasitoid wasps and triggers leaf-miner resurgence.

description.en: *Liriomyza sativae* is recorded in Sri Lanka with very high field infestation rates — 89% on tomato and 98% on mung bean in surveys. Adults are small black-and-yellow flies; females puncture leaves to feed and lay eggs. Larvae tunnel between the upper and lower leaf epidermis, creating the characteristic serpentine whitish mines; heavy mining reduces photosynthesis, causes leaf drop, and stunts plants. **DOA-recommended management**: yellow sticky traps, row covers to exclude adult flies, removal of alternate weed hosts, prompt destruction of infested leaves. Chemical options cited by DOA: Azadirachtin 1% EC at 16 ml per 16 L water, Abamectin 18 g/L EC at 9.6 ml per 16 L, neem seed water extract at 640 g per 16 L; Spinetoram has shown the highest reduction 7 days after application.

description.quote: Liriomyza sativae prefers hosts in the Cucurbitaceae, Fabaceae and Solanaceae families. Tomato showed 89% infestation and mung bean 98% infestation in field studies. DOA recommends Azadiractin 1% EC at 16ml/16 liters of water, Abamectin 18g/l EC at 9.6 ml/16 liters of water, and neem seed water extract at 640g/16 liters of water. Insecticidal effects showed the highest reduction values were observed after 7 DOA for Spinetoram
